root_text.an9.35:1.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, gāvī pabbateyyā bālā abyattā akhettaññū akusalā visame pabbate carituṁ.
root_text.an9.35:1.3: ‘yannūnāhaṁ agatapubbañceva disaṁ gaccheyyaṁ, akhāditapubbāni ca tiṇāni khādeyyaṁ, apītapubbāni ca pānīyāni piveyyan’ti.
root_text.an9.35:1.4: Sā purimaṁ pādaṁ na suppatiṭṭhitaṁ patiṭṭhāpetvā pacchimaṁ pādaṁ uddhareyya.
root_text.an9.35:1.5: Sā na ceva agatapubbaṁ disaṁ gaccheyya, na ca akhāditapubbāni tiṇāni khādeyya, na ca apītapubbāni pānīyāni piveyya;
root_text.an9.35:1.7: ‘yannūnāhaṁ agatapubbañceva disaṁ gaccheyyaṁ, akhāditapubbāni ca tiṇāni khādeyyaṁ, apītapubbāni ca pānīyāni piveyyan’ti tañca padesaṁ na sotthinā paccāgaccheyya.
root_text.an9.35:1.9: Tathā hi sā, bhikkhave, gāvī pabbateyyā bālā abyattā akhettaññū akusalā visame pabbate carituṁ.
root_text.an9.35:1.10: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, idhekacco bhikkhu bālo abyatto akhettaññū akusalo vivicceva kāmehi vivicca akusalehi dhammehi savitakkaṁ savicāraṁ vivekajaṁ pītisukhaṁ paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati;
root_text.an9.35:1.11: so taṁ nimittaṁ na āsevati na bhāveti na bahulīkaroti na svādhiṭṭhitaṁ adhiṭṭhāti.
root_text.an9.35:2.2: ‘yannūnāhaṁ vitakkavicārānaṁ vūpasamā ajjhattaṁ sampasādanaṁ cetaso ekodibhāvaṁ avitakkaṁ avicāraṁ samādhijaṁ pītisukhaṁ dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja vihareyyan’ti.
root_text.an9.35:2.3: So na sakkoti vitakkavicārānaṁ vūpasamā …pe… dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharituṁ.
root_text.an9.35:2.5: ‘yannūnāhaṁ vivicceva kāmehi vivicca akusalehi dhammehi savitakkaṁ savicāraṁ vivekajaṁ pītisukhaṁ paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja vihareyyan’ti.
root_text.an9.35:2.6: So na sakkoti vivicceva kāmehi …pe… paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharituṁ.
root_text.an9.35:2.7: Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, ‘bhikkhu ubhato bhaṭṭho ubhato parihīno, seyyathāpi sā gāvī pabbateyyā bālā abyattā akhettaññū akusalā visame pabbate carituṁ’.
root_text.an9.35:3.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, gāvī pabbateyyā paṇḍitā byattā khettaññū kusalā visame pabbate carituṁ.
root_text.an9.35:3.3: ‘yannūnāhaṁ agatapubbañceva disaṁ gaccheyyaṁ, akhāditapubbāni ca tiṇāni khādeyyaṁ, apītapubbāni ca pānīyāni piveyyan’ti.
root_text.an9.35:3.4: Sā purimaṁ pādaṁ suppatiṭṭhitaṁ patiṭṭhāpetvā pacchimaṁ pādaṁ uddhareyya.
root_text.an9.35:3.5: Sā agatapubbañceva disaṁ gaccheyya, akhāditapubbāni ca tiṇāni khādeyya, apītapubbāni ca pānīyāni piveyya.
root_text.an9.35:3.7: ‘yannūnāhaṁ agatapubbañceva disaṁ gaccheyyaṁ, akhāditapubbāni ca tiṇāni khādeyyaṁ, apītapubbāni ca pānīyāni piveyyan’ti tañca padesaṁ sotthinā paccāgaccheyya.
root_text.an9.35:3.9: Tathā hi sā, bhikkhave, gāvī pabbateyyā paṇḍitā byattā khettaññū kusalā visame pabbate carituṁ.
root_text.an9.35:3.10: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, idhekacco bhikkhu paṇḍito byatto khettaññū kusalo vivicceva kāmehi vivicca akusalehi dhammehi savitakkaṁ savicāraṁ vivekajaṁ pītisukhaṁ paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an9.35:3.11: So taṁ nimittaṁ āsevati bhāveti bahulīkaroti svādhiṭṭhitaṁ adhiṭṭhāti.
root_text.an9.35:4.2: ‘yannūnāhaṁ vitakkavicārānaṁ vūpasamā ajjhattaṁ sampasādanaṁ cetaso ekodibhāvaṁ avitakkaṁ avicāraṁ samādhijaṁ pītisukhaṁ dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja vihareyyan’ti.
root_text.an9.35:4.3: So dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ anabhihiṁsamāno vitakkavicārānaṁ vūpasamā … dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an9.35:4.4: So taṁ nimittaṁ āsevati bhāveti bahulīkaroti svādhiṭṭhitaṁ adhiṭṭhāti.
root_text.an9.35:5.2: ‘yannūnāhaṁ pītiyā ca virāgā upekkhako ca vihareyyaṁ sato ca sampajāno, sukhañca kāyena paṭisaṁvedeyyaṁ yaṁ taṁ ariyā ācikkhanti—upekkhako satimā sukhavihārīti tatiyaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja vihareyyan’ti.
root_text.an9.35:5.3: So tatiyaṁ jhānaṁ anabhihiṁsamāno pītiyā ca virāgā … tatiyaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an9.35:5.4: So taṁ nimittaṁ āsevati bhāveti bahulīkaroti svādhiṭṭhitaṁ adhiṭṭhāti.
root_text.an9.35:6.2: ‘yannūnāhaṁ sukhassa ca pahānā dukkhassa ca pahānā pubbeva somanassadomanassānaṁ atthaṅgamā adukkhamasukhaṁ upekkhāsatipārisuddhiṁ catutthaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja vihareyyan’ti.
root_text.an9.35:6.3: So catutthaṁ jhānaṁ anabhihiṁsamāno sukhassa ca pahānā …pe… catutthaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an9.35:6.4: So taṁ nimittaṁ āsevati bhāveti bahulīkaroti svādhiṭṭhitaṁ adhiṭṭhāti.
root_text.an9.35:7.2: ‘yannūnāhaṁ sabbaso rūpasaññānaṁ samatikkamā paṭighasaññānaṁ atthaṅgamā nānattasaññānaṁ amanasikārā ananto ākāsoti ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ upasampajja vihareyyan’ti.
root_text.an9.35:7.3: So ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ anabhihiṁsamāno sabbaso rūpasaññānaṁ samatikkamā …pe… ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an9.35:7.4: So taṁ nimittaṁ āsevati bhāveti bahulīkaroti svādhiṭṭhitaṁ adhiṭṭhāti.
root_text.an9.35:8.2: ‘yannūnāhaṁ sabbaso ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ samatikkamma anantaṁ viññāṇanti viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ upasampajja vihareyyan’ti.
root_text.an9.35:8.3: So viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ anabhihiṁsamāno sabbaso ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ samatikkamma ‘anantaṁ viññāṇan’ti viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an9.35:8.4: So taṁ nimittaṁ āsevati bhāveti bahulīkaroti svādhiṭṭhitaṁ adhiṭṭhāti.
root_text.an9.35:9.2: ‘yannūnāhaṁ sabbaso viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ samatikkamma natthi kiñcīti ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ upasampajja vihareyyan’ti.
root_text.an9.35:9.3: So ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ anabhihiṁsamāno sabbaso viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ samatikkamma ‘natthi kiñcī’ti ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an9.35:9.4: So taṁ nimittaṁ āsevati bhāveti bahulīkaroti svādhiṭṭhitaṁ adhiṭṭhāti.
root_text.an9.35:10.2: ‘yannūnāhaṁ sabbaso ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ samatikkamma nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ upasampajja vihareyyan’ti.
root_text.an9.35:10.3: So nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ anabhihiṁsamāno sabbaso ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ samatikkamma nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an9.35:10.4: So taṁ nimittaṁ āsevati bhāveti bahulīkaroti svādhiṭṭhitaṁ adhiṭṭhāti.
root_text.an9.35:11.2: ‘yannūnāhaṁ sabbaso nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ samatikkamma saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ upasampajja vihareyyan’ti.
root_text.an9.35:11.3: So saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ anabhihiṁsamāno sabbaso nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ samatikkamma saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an9.35:12.1: Yato kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu taṁ tadeva samāpattiṁ samāpajjatipi vuṭṭhātipi, tassa mudu cittaṁ hoti kammaññaṁ.
root_text.an9.35:12.2: Mudunā kammaññena cittena appamāṇo samādhi hoti subhāvito.
root_text.an9.35:12.3: So appamāṇena samādhinā subhāvitena yassa yassa abhiññāsacchikaraṇīyassa dhammassa cittaṁ abhininnāmeti abhiññāsacchikiriyāya tatra tatreva sakkhibhabbataṁ pāpuṇāti sati sati āyatane.
root_text.an9.35:13.1: So sace ākaṅkhati: ‘anekavihitaṁ iddhividhaṁ paccanubhaveyyaṁ—ekopi hutvā bahudhā assaṁ, bahudhāpi hutvā eko assaṁ …pe… yāva brahmalokāpi kāyena vasaṁ vatteyyan’ti,
root_text.an9.35:13.2: tatra tatreva sakkhibhabbataṁ pāpuṇāti sati sati āyatane.
root_text.an9.35:15.1: So sace ākaṅkhati: ‘parasattānaṁ parapuggalānaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajāneyyaṁ,
root_text.an9.35:15.2: sarāgaṁ vā cittaṁ sarāgaṁ cittanti pajāneyyaṁ,
root_text.an9.35:15.3: vītarāgaṁ vā cittaṁ vītarāgaṁ cittanti pajāneyyaṁ,
root_text.an9.35:15.4: sadosaṁ vā cittaṁ sadosaṁ cittanti pajāneyyaṁ,
root_text.an9.35:15.5: vītadosaṁ vā cittaṁ vītadosaṁ cittanti pajāneyyaṁ,
root_text.an9.35:15.6: samohaṁ vā cittaṁ samohaṁ cittanti pajāneyyaṁ,
root_text.an9.35:15.17: avimuttaṁ vā cittaṁ avimuttaṁ cittanti pajāneyyan’ti,
root_text.an9.35:15.18: tatra tatreva sakkhibhabbataṁ pāpuṇāti sati sati āyatane.
root_text.an9.35:16.1: So sace ākaṅkhati: ‘anekavihitaṁ pubbenivāsaṁ anussareyyaṁ, seyyathidaṁ—ekampi jātiṁ dvepi jātiyo …pe… iti sākāraṁ sauddesaṁ anekavihitaṁ pubbenivāsaṁ anussareyyan’ti,
root_text.an9.35:16.2: tatra tatreva sakkhibhabbataṁ pāpuṇāti sati sati āyatane.
root_text.an9.35:17.1: So sace ākaṅkhati: ‘dibbena cakkhunā visuddhena atikkantamānusakena …pe… yathākammūpage satte pajāneyyan’ti
root_text.an9.35:17.2: tatra tatreva sakkhibhabbataṁ pāpuṇāti sati sati āyatane.
root_text.an9.35:18.1: So sace ākaṅkhati: ‘āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja vihareyyan’ti,
root_text.an9.35:18.2: tatra tatreva sakkhibhabbataṁ pāpuṇāti sati sati āyatane”ti.

translation_text.an9.35:1.1: “Mendicants, suppose there was a mountain cow who was foolish, incompetent, unskillful, and lacked common sense when roaming on rugged mountains.
translation_text.an9.35:1.3: ‘Why don’t I go somewhere I’ve never been before? I could eat grass and drink water that I’ve never tried before.’
translation_text.an9.35:1.4: She’d take a step with a fore-hoof; but before it was properly set down, she’d lift up a hind-hoof.
translation_text.an9.35:1.5: She wouldn’t go somewhere she’d never been before, or eat grass and drink water that she’d never tried before.
translation_text.an9.35:1.6: And she’d never return safely to the place she had started from.
translation_text.an9.35:1.9: Because that mountain cow was foolish, incompetent, unskillful, and lacked common sense when roaming on rugged mountains.
translation_text.an9.35:1.10: In the same way, some foolish, incompetent, unskillful mendicant, lacking common sense, quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unskillful qualities, enters and remains in the first absorption, which has the rapture and bliss born of seclusion, while placing the mind and keeping it connected.
translation_text.an9.35:1.11: But they don’t cultivate, develop, and make much of that basis; they don’t ensure it is properly stabilized.
translation_text.an9.35:2.2: ‘Why don’t I, as the placing of the mind and keeping it connected are stilled, enter and remain in the second absorption, which has the rapture and bliss born of immersion, with internal clarity and mind at one, without placing the mind and keeping it connected.’
translation_text.an9.35:2.3: But they’re not able to enter and remain in the second absorption.
translation_text.an9.35:2.5: ‘Why don’t I, quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unskillful qualities, enter and remain in the first absorption, which has the rapture and bliss born of seclusion, while placing the mind and keeping it connected.’
translation_text.an9.35:2.6: But they’re not able to enter and remain in the first absorption.
translation_text.an9.35:2.7: This is called a mendicant who has slipped and fallen from both sides. They’re like the mountain cow who was foolish, incompetent, unskillful, and lacking in common sense when roaming on rugged mountains.
translation_text.an9.35:3.1: Suppose there was a mountain cow who was astute, competent, skillful, and used common sense when roaming on rugged mountains.
translation_text.an9.35:3.3: ‘Why don’t I go somewhere I’ve never been before? I could eat grass and drink water that I’ve never tried before.’
translation_text.an9.35:3.4: She’d take a step with a fore-hoof; and after it was properly set down, she’d lift up a hind-hoof.
translation_text.an9.35:3.5: She’d go somewhere she’d never been before, and eat grass and drink water that she’d never tried before.
translation_text.an9.35:3.6: And she’d return safely to the place she had started from.
translation_text.an9.35:3.9: Because that mountain cow was astute, competent, skillful, and used common sense when roaming on rugged mountains.
translation_text.an9.35:3.10: In the same way, some astute, competent, skillful mendicant, using common sense, quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unskillful qualities, enters and remains in the first absorption, which has the rapture and bliss born of seclusion, while placing the mind and keeping it connected.
translation_text.an9.35:3.11: They cultivate, develop, and make much of that basis, ensuring that it’s properly stabilized.
translation_text.an9.35:4.2: ‘Why don’t I, as the placing of the mind and keeping it connected are stilled, enter and remain in the second absorption, which has the rapture and bliss born of immersion, with internal clarity and mind at one, without placing the mind and keeping it connected.’
translation_text.an9.35:4.3: Without charging at the second absorption, as the placing of the mind and keeping it connected are stilled, they enter and remain in the second absorption.
translation_text.an9.35:4.4: They cultivate, develop, and make much of that basis, ensuring that it’s properly stabilized.
translation_text.an9.35:5.2: ‘Why don’t I, with the fading away of rapture, enter and remain in the third absorption, where I will meditate with equanimity, mindful and aware, personally experiencing the bliss of which the noble ones declare, “Equanimous and mindful, one meditates in bliss.”’
translation_text.an9.35:5.3: Without charging at the third absorption, with the fading away of rapture, they enter and remain in the third absorption.
translation_text.an9.35:5.4: They cultivate, develop, and make much of that basis, ensuring that it’s properly stabilized.
translation_text.an9.35:6.2: ‘Why don’t I, with the giving up of pleasure and pain, and the disappearance of former happiness and sadness, enter and remain in the fourth absorption, without pleasure or pain, with pure equanimity and mindfulness.’
translation_text.an9.35:6.3: Without charging at the fourth absorption, with the giving up of pleasure and pain, and the ending of former happiness and sadness, they enter and remain in the fourth absorption.
translation_text.an9.35:6.4: They cultivate, develop, and make much of that basis, ensuring that it’s properly stabilized.
translation_text.an9.35:7.2: ‘Why don’t I, going totally beyond perceptions of form, with the disappearance of perceptions of impingement, not focusing on perceptions of diversity, aware that “space is infinite”, enter and remain in the dimension of infinite space.’
translation_text.an9.35:7.3: Without charging at the dimension of infinite space, with the fading away of rapture, they enter and remain in the dimension of infinite space.
translation_text.an9.35:7.4: They cultivate, develop, and make much of that basis, ensuring that it’s properly stabilized.
translation_text.an9.35:8.2: ‘Why don’t I, going totally beyond the dimension of infinite space, aware that “consciousness is infinite”, enter and remain in the dimension of infinite consciousness.’
translation_text.an9.35:8.3: Without charging at the dimension of infinite consciousness, they enter and remain in the dimension of infinite consciousness.
translation_text.an9.35:8.4: They cultivate, develop, and make much of that basis, ensuring that it’s properly stabilized.
translation_text.an9.35:9.2: ‘Why don’t I, going totally beyond the dimension of infinite consciousness, aware that “there is nothing at all”, enter and remain in the dimension of nothingness.’
translation_text.an9.35:9.3: Without charging at the dimension of nothingness, they enter and remain in the dimension of nothingness.
translation_text.an9.35:9.4: They cultivate, develop, and make much of that basis, ensuring that it’s properly stabilized.
translation_text.an9.35:10.2: ‘Why don’t I, going totally beyond the dimension of nothingness, enter and remain in the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception.’
translation_text.an9.35:10.3: Without charging at the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception, they enter and remain in the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception.
translation_text.an9.35:10.4: They cultivate, develop, and make much of that basis, ensuring that it’s properly stabilized.
translation_text.an9.35:11.2: ‘Why don’t I, going totally beyond the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception, enter and remain in the cessation of perception and feeling.’
translation_text.an9.35:11.3: Without charging at the cessation of perception and feeling, they enter and remain in the cessation of perception and feeling.
translation_text.an9.35:12.1: When a mendicant enters and emerges from all these attainments, their mind becomes pliable and workable.
translation_text.an9.35:12.2: With a pliable and workable mind, their immersion becomes limitless and well developed.
translation_text.an9.35:12.3: They extend the mind to realize by insight each and every thing that can be realized by insight; and they are capable of realizing those things, since each and every one is within range.
translation_text.an9.35:13.1: They might wish: ‘May I wield the many kinds of psychic power: multiplying myself and becoming one again … controlling my body as far as the realm of divinity.’
translation_text.an9.35:13.2: They are capable of realizing it, since each and every one is within range.
translation_text.an9.35:14.1: They might wish: ‘With clairaudience that is purified and superhuman, may I hear both kinds of sounds, human and heavenly, whether near or far.’
translation_text.an9.35:14.2: They are capable of realizing it, since each and every one is within range.
translation_text.an9.35:15.1: They might wish: ‘May I understand the minds of other beings and individuals, having encompassed them with my mind.
translation_text.an9.35:15.2: May I understand mind with greed as “mind with greed”,
translation_text.an9.35:15.3: and mind without greed as “mind without greed”;
translation_text.an9.35:15.4: mind with hate as “mind with hate”,
translation_text.an9.35:15.5: and mind without hate as “mind without hate”;
translation_text.an9.35:15.6: mind with delusion as “mind with delusion”,
translation_text.an9.35:15.7: and mind without delusion as “mind without delusion”;
translation_text.an9.35:15.18: They are capable of realizing it, since each and every one is within range.
translation_text.an9.35:16.1: They might wish: ‘May I recollect many kinds of past lives. That is: one, two, three, four, five, ten, twenty, thirty, forty, fifty, a hundred, a thousand, a hundred thousand rebirths; many eons of the world contracting, many eons of the world expanding, many eons of the world contracting and expanding. They remember: “There, I was named this, my clan was that, I looked like this, and that was my food. This was how I felt pleasure and pain, and that was how my life ended. When I passed away from that place I was reborn somewhere else. There, too, I was named this, my clan was that, I looked like this, and that was my food. This was how I felt pleasure and pain, and that was how my life ended. When I passed away from that place I was reborn here.” May I recollect my many past lives, with features and details.’
translation_text.an9.35:16.2: They’re capable of realizing it, since each and every one is within range.
translation_text.an9.35:17.1: They might wish: ‘With clairvoyance that is purified and superhuman, may I see sentient beings passing away and being reborn—inferior and superior, beautiful and ugly, in a good place or a bad place—and understand how sentient beings pass on according to their deeds.’
translation_text.an9.35:17.2: They’re capable of realizing it, since each and every one is within range.
translation_text.an9.35:18.1: They might wish: ‘May I realize the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life, and live having realized it with my own insight due to the ending of defilements.’
translation_text.an9.35:18.2: They’re capable of realizing it, since each and every one is within range.”
